title: Church of Our Lady of the Snows
tagline: Church of Our Lady of the Snows — hill station of Goa

heroImage.alt: Church of Our Lady of the Snows, Goa

overview.short: The Igreja de Nossa Senhora das Neves (Church of Our Lady of the Snows) is a historical Catholic church in Rachol village, Salcete sub-district, on the southern banks of the Zuari
overview.description: The Igreja de Nossa Senhora das Neves (Church of Our Lady of the Snows) is a historical Catholic church in Rachol village, Salcete sub-district, on the southern banks of the Zuari river, in the South Goa district of Goa state, India. The church was built in the 1560's during the Goa Inquisition. It is situated in close proximity to the renowned Rachol Seminary. There is a church of the same name in the neighbouring village of Raia.

topPlaces.name: Rachol
topPlaces.description: Rachol (also known as Raitura) is a prominent village in the Salcete taluka (subdistrict) of South Goa district, Goa state, peninsular India. It is located on the left bank of the Zuari River and is home to the famous Rachol Seminary.

topPlaces.name: Rachol Fort
topPlaces.description: The Rachol Fort is a historical Portuguese era fort located in the village of Rachol, Salcette concelho, in the state of Goa on the west coast of India.

topPlaces.name: D'Mello House
topPlaces.description: D'Mello House is an abandoned mansion that was built during the Portuguese rule in Goa. It is located in Santimol, Raia, and is described as one of the most haunted places in Goa.

topPlaces.name: Igorchem Bandh
topPlaces.description: Igorchem Bandh (transl. Church Road) is a road situated in the village of Raia, adjacent to Rachol, within the coastal state of Goa. It has gained attention for its association with midday hauntings.

topPlaces.name: Shiroda, Goa
topPlaces.description: Shiroda is a village in Ponda Taluka in South Goa District, Goa, India. The village has a population of 14,112 (Male: 6,928 Female: 7,184) based on 2001 census data.

topPlaces.name: Curtorim
topPlaces.description: Curtorim is a town in the Salcette taluka of South Goa district in Goa, India. It comes under Margao metropolitan region.

topPlaces.name: Loutolim
topPlaces.description: Loutolim or Loutulim (Konkani: Lottli pronounced:[lɔːʈlĩː], Portuguese: Loutulim) is a large village of South Goa district in the state of Goa, India. It is in the Salcete sub-district.

topPlaces.name: Arlem Breweries Ground
topPlaces.description: Arlem Breweries Ground is a cricket ground in Margao, Goa, India. Established in 1976, the ground held its first first-class to be played there came in the 1985/86 Ranji Trophy when Goa played Hyderabad.

items.note: Rachol (also known as Raitura) is a prominent village in the Salcete taluka (subdistrict) of South Goa district, Goa state, peninsular India. It is located on the left bank of the Zuari River and is home to the famous Rachol Seminary.
The Rachol Fort is a historical Portuguese era fort located in the village of Rachol, Salcette concelho, in the state of Goa on the west coast of India.
D'Mello House is an abandoned mansion that was built during the Portuguese rule in Goa. It is located in Santimol, Raia, and is described as one of the most haunted places in Goa.
Igorchem Bandh (transl. Church Road) is a road situated in the village of Raia, adjacent to Rachol, within the coastal state of Goa. It has gained attention for its association with midday hauntings.
Shiroda is a village in Ponda Taluka in South Goa District, Goa, India. The village has a population of 14,112 (Male: 6,928 Female: 7,184) based on 2001 census data.
Curtorim is a town in the Salcette taluka of South Goa district in Goa, India. It comes under Margao metropolitan region.
Loutolim or Loutulim (Konkani: Lottli pronounced:[lɔːʈlĩː], Portuguese: Loutulim) is a large village of South Goa district in the state of Goa, India. It is in the Salcete sub-district.

Arlem Breweries Ground is a cricket ground in Margao, Goa, India. Established in 1976, the ground held its first first-class to be played there came in the 1985/86 Ranji Trophy when Goa played Hyderabad.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Church of Our Lady of the Snows?
faq.a: The best time to visit Church of Our Lady of the Snows is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Church of Our Lady of the Snows?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (21 km) and the nearest railway station is Madgaon Junction (Goa) (7 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Church of Our Lady of the Snows?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Church of Our Lady of the Snows at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Church of Our Lady of the Snows?
faq.a: Stays in Church of Our Lady of the Snows start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Church of Our Lady of the Snows?
faq.a: The top attractions are Rachol, Rachol Fort, D'Mello House, Igorchem Bandh, Shiroda, Goa.
